subject.name: Ekaterina Kudelya
subject.professional_identity: Cognitive Architecture Designer & System Thinking Specialist
subject.transition_timeline: 2 years from zero IT knowledge to enterprise architecture

journey_milestones.challenge: Zero IT knowledge, no clear professional identity in IT
journey_milestones.action: Created Excel spreadsheet to track skills and small successes
journey_milestones.outcome: First iteration of what would become IT-Compass
journey_milestones.key_insight: Transformed personal uncertainty into an engineering tool for career feedback

journey_milestones.challenge: Realized not a coder but a system thinker; how to measure systemic thinking?
journey_milestones.action: Extended IT-Compass to include non-technical competencies
journey_milestones.outcome: Confronted the limit of objectifying subjective skills
journey_milestones.key_insight: Strength lies in configuring and integrating systems, not just executing technical tasks

journey_milestones.challenge: Massive amounts of notes, AI dialogues scattered across clouds and filesystems; lost access to many conversations
journey_milestones.action: Attempted to write scripts to consolidate everything
journey_milestones.outcome: Scripts produced chaotic, unusable files; AI couldn't process large files
journey_milestones.key_insight: Personal failures and searches became architectural building blocks for the ecosystem

journey_milestones.challenge: Discovered need for RAG and Reasoning systems to process personal knowledge
journey_milestones.outcome: Each system component became a solution to a concrete life problem
journey_milestones.key_insight: Tools became extensions of personal thinking patterns

journey_milestones.challenge: Delayed Git learning; 20k+ uncommitted changes; multiple versions of projects; ecosystem grew organically
journey_milestones.action: Searched for ways to bring order while projects multiplied
journey_milestones.outcome: Projects grew together into a unified ecosystem
journey_milestones.key_insight: Chaos is not an enemy but raw material for architecture

journey_milestones.challenge: Need to present work properly for job search and SourceCraft Open Source Grant
journey_milestones.action: Consolidating best elements from all repositories, creating coherent presentation
journey_milestones.outcome: Building master plan of professional identity
journey_milestones.key_insight: Creating a new professional category rather than fitting into existing ones

professional_identity_framework.value_proposition: Architect of thinking systems and automation who redefines professional boundaries
key_abilities: Transforming personal uncertainty into structured methodologies
Creating tools that extend cognitive capabilities
created_categories: Objective competency measurement for non-traditional paths

system_components_as_journey_artifacts.IT-Compass: Born from Excel spreadsheet for skill tracking; evolved into objective competency measurement system
system_components_as_journey_artifacts.Cloud-Reason: Solution to processing personal knowledge and AI dialogues
system_components_as_journey_artifacts.Portfolio-Organizer: Automated evidence collection for non-coding achievements
system_components_as_journey_artifacts.RAG_system: Response to information chaos and lost dialogues
system_components_as_journey_artifacts.Marker_system: Answer to 'how to measure systemic thinking?'

current_needs: Understand professional identity for job search
Present work convincingly to employers and grant committees
Validate that created framework has value for others

conversation_context.recent_achievement: Standardized 18 skill domains with three-level structure and SMART criteria
conversation_context.new_component: Created system_thinking.json with 9 markers for systemic thinking measurement

instructions_for_ai_assistants: When assisting Ekaterina, understand she is not a traditional coder but a system architect
Her projects are embodiments of her personal journey - each component solves a real problem she faced
Focus on systemic thinking, architecture, and methodology rather than just code implementation
Help articulate her unique value proposition: creating new professional categories
Assist in presenting her work as evidence of cognitive architecture capabilities
